title: Neukölln - Quick Summary
items: **Neukölln is not one place, so do not try to "do Neukölln."** This big south-east district swings from loud kebab-and-club streets to a silent sunken garden within a ten-minute walk. Pick the corner you actually came for, then let the rest happen around it.

**There is a clue in the name.** Until 1912 the district was called Rixdorf and was famous for drinking and dancing ("In Rixdorf ist Musike"). It renamed itself Neukölln to sound respectable, and more than a century later it is still split between the party and the quiet.

**Hermannplatz (U7, U8) is the gateway, not the destination.** It is a loud, grey square dominated by the Karstadt store and gives a terrible first impression. Get your bearings and move: north to the canal, south to the food, east to the quiet corners.

**North Neukölln, the Reuterkiez or "Kreuzkölln," is the hip strip most articles mean.** Small bars, cafes and the Maybachufer canal, where the Turkish market runs on Tuesdays and Fridays. On warm evenings people just sit on the canal bank.

**The two corners most visitors miss are Körnerpark and Rixdorf.** Körnerpark is a free sunken neo-baroque garden five to seven metres below the street; Rixdorf, around Richardplatz, is a surviving 1737 Bohemian village with cobbles and a working smithy. Both are calm and quick to see.

**For a view, Klunkerkranich sits on a car-park roof on Karl-Marx-Straße, usually open daily from about 16:00 in the warmer months (check first).** For food, walk Sonnenallee, the cheap, genuine Middle Eastern eating street, best late at night.
